Directions: Read the following statements and choose the correct answer from choices A-D below. Statement 1: A requision has more stringent requirements than a purchase order. Statement 2: The issuance of a purchase order means the department acknowledges there are funds available to purchase the item. Statement 3: Bid specifications are general guidelines that vendors use to justify their products.

Directions: Read the following statements regarding an annual performance evaluation, then select your answer from alternatives A-D below. Statement 1: The officer should begin an evaluation session by explaining that an evaluation is a tool to help document how well the employee is performing their duties. Statement 2: The session should be informal. Statement 3: The annual evaluation should include a discussion of future goals and objectives.

Directions: Read the following statements regarding annual employee evaluations and select the correct answer from choices A through D below. Statement 1: The completion of the annual evaluation forms should be viewed as a formality. Statement 2: The information that the evaluator enters into the form should not come as a surprise to the employee. Statement 3: The scheduled evaluation should be used primarily as an opportunity to discuss future goals and objectives.

Directions: Read the following statements regarding discipline or termination of an employee, and select your answer from choices A through D. Statement 1: Discipline or termination proceedings may be justified when it becomes apparent that an employee is unable or unwilling to meet the minimum employment standard. Statement 2: Failure to discipline or terminate an employee can create an unsafe environment for other employees within the workforce. Statement 3: Discipline should not be remedial.

Directions: Read the following statements regarding discipline or termination of an employee, and select your answer from choices A through D. Statement 1: Discipline or termination proceedings may be justified when it becomes apparent that an employee is unable or unwilling to meet the minimum employment standard. Statement 2: Failure to discipline or terminate an employee can create an unsafe environment within the workforce. Statement 3: Discipline should not be remedial.

Directions: Read the following statements regarding fire department purchasing and select your answer from choices A-D. Statement 1: Failure of officers to know and understand purchasing policies and procedures can have significant consequences for the fire department. Statement 2: Purchasing violations are usually found during the audit, review, revision process. Statement 3: Because fire departments are either political entities or nonprofit organizations, vendors are required to accept purchase orders.

Directions: Read the following statements regarding fire department purchasing and select your answer from choices A-D. Statement 1: Typically, once the petty cash limit is exceeded by the cost of an item, the purchase order system is used. Statement 2: Like an operation fund, the purchase order system elimates the need to secure price quotes before purchase. Statement 3: Any fire department member can authorize a purchase order with their signature.

Directions: Read the following statements regarding fire department purchasing and select your answer from choices A-D. Statement 1: Typically, once the petty cash limit is exceeded by the cost of an item, the purchase order system is used. Statement 2: Like an operation fund, the purchase order system eliminates the need to secure price quotes before purchase. Statement 3: All three statements are true.

Directions: Read the following statements regarding fire department purchasing and select your answer from choices A-D. Directions: Read the following statements regarding rules, procedures, and guideline changes and then select your answer from choices A-D below: Statement 1: Groups working together usually have their own set of informal rules, guidelines, and procedures. Statement 2: Informal rules, guidelines, and procedures can be much stronger or persuasive than formal ones. Statement 3: When the company officer is recommending a change in rules, guidelines, and procedures, the group's informal ones should be considered.

Directions: Read the following statements regarding work performance, then select your answer from alternatives A-D below. Statement 1: Most performance evaluation systems requrie a formal review of each member's work performance on at least an annual basis. Statement 2: In a formal review, the quality of the work, rather than the quantity of work, is evaluated. Statement 3: The formal review focuses on poor performance.

Directions: Read the following statements then select your answer from alternatives A-D below. Statement 1: The incident review process may include recommendations for changes in procedures. Statement 2: The incident review process shall be conducted using a free-lance management style. Statement 3: The incident review shall include all company officers, not just those at the actual incident.

Directions: Read the following statements, then select your answer from choices A-D below. Statement 1: An essential element of a group is that the members have a common binding interest. Statement 2: Any member in a group may emerge as the leader. Statement 3: Leaders of an informal group are usually appointed.
